sourceStatus.publicationPolicy: No long SEC source text is embedded; evidence uses short digests and source URL anchors.

keyPoints.text: 3분기 매출이 전년비 10% 증가했고, GBV는 전년비 14% 성장했습니다.
순이익률 34%, 조정 EBITDA 마진 50%를 기록하며 수익성이 크게 개선됐습니다.
숙박 및 체험 예약 건수는 전년비 9% 증가했으며, 미국 시장 강세와 ADR 성장이 수요를 이끌었습니다.

businessChanges.text: 신규 취소 정책을 도입해 게스트는 예약 확정 후 24시간 내 전액 환불이 가능하고, 호스트는 체크인 14일 전까지 무료 취소 옵션을 선택할 수 있습니다.
국제 확장 전략을 다년간 추진 중이며, 핵심 이외 시장 야간 예약 건수 성장률이 평균을 상회하는 성과를 내고 있다고 밝혔습니다.
상품 개선, 고객 유치, 국제 확장, AI 통합 등 핵심 성장 전략을 지속 추진하고 있다고 경영진이 밝혔습니다.

financialHighlights.text: 잉여현금흐름(FCF) 마진은 33%, 향후 12개월 누적 FCF 마진은 38%를 기록했습니다.
GBV는 환율 제외 기준으로 전년비 12% 성장했습니다.
매출은 환율 제외 기준으로도 전년비 10% 성장하며 안정적인 성장세를 나타냈습니다.

watchItems.text: 50% 조정 EBITDA 마진이라는 높은 수익성이 성수기 이후에도 유지될지가 핵심 체크 포인트입니다.
국제 시장 확장 속도와 신규 취소 정책이 호스트·게스트 양측의 만족도와 예약 전환율에 미치는 영향을 모니터링해야 합니다.

evidence.sourceTextDigest: EX-99.1 2 d40503dex991.htm EX-99.1 EX-99.1 Exhibit 99.1 Q3 2025 Revenue Key Financial $4.1B Measures 10% Y/Y 10% Y/Y (ex-FX) Net Income $1.4B 34% Net income margin Adjusted EBITDA* $2.1B 50% Adjusted EBITDA Margin Free Cash $1.3B Flow (“FCF”)* 33% FCF Margin Trailing Twelve Month $4.5B (“TTM”) FCF* 38% TTM FCF Margin Gross Booking Key Business $22.9B Value (“GBV”) Metrics 14% Y/Y 12% Y/Y (ex-FX) Nights and 133.6M Seats Booked 9% Y/Y *A reconciliation of non-GAAP financial measures to the most comparable GAAP measures is provided at the end of this letter. 2 Q3 2025 Shareholder Letter Q3 2025 was another strong quarter for Airbnb. Revenue increased 10% year-over-year, landing at the high end of our guidance range. Adjusted EBITDA was over $2.0 billion, our highest in any quarter. Gross Booking Value increased 14% year-over-year, while  Normalized numeric aliases: $2.0 billion = 20억 달러; $4.1 = 4.1달러; $1.4 = 1.4달러; $2.1 = 2.1달러; $1.3 = 1.3달러; $4.5 = 4.5달러; $22.9 = 22.9달러; $2.0 = 2.0달러.

from Q2 and exceeded our expectations, driven by the strength in the U.S. market and ADR. We’re driving continued growth by focusing on four key areas: making our service better, bringing Airbnb to more parts of the world, expanding what we offer, and integrating AI into our app. Here are some of the highlights: We’re making Airbnb better for hosts and guests: This quarter, we announced dozens of new features that make it easier to book the perfect stay, manage your reservation, and discover more options in great locations. Here are a few of them: ¦ Reserve Now, Pay Later—We know guests want more flexible ways to pay, so we recently introduced a feature in the U.S. that gives guests the option to reserve an eligible stay and pay $0 upfront. This helped drive the acceleration of Nights and Seats Booked in Q3, and we’ll continue rolling it out more broadly in the coming months. ¦  Normalized numeric aliases: $0 = 0달러.

cancellation policies—We’re helping hosts earn more and guests make changes more easily by updating our cancellation policies. Hosts can now choose a new policy that allows guests to cancel for free up to 14 days before check-in. And for all stays under 28 nights, guests can cancel for a full refund up to 24 hours after their reservation is confirmed, if they book more than 7 days before check-in. Initial tests show that these changes are reducing customer service cases and increasing annualized bookings. ¦ Improved maps—When guests are looking at a potential Airbnb, they’ll see nearby landmarks, public transit, restaurants and more. Later this year, guests will also be able to see different map views, including satellite, street, and transit. ¦ Flexible carousels—When guests search for a place to stay, they’ll see homes just outside their search criteria—like places with similar

different amenities, or stays in nearby 1 towns for less. The goal is to drive bookings by making it easier for guests to find amazing stays they might have otherwise missed. We’re bringing Airbnb to more parts of the world: International expansion continues to be a significant growth opportunity for us, and the team is executing against a multi-year strategy that’s already off to a strong start. The average growth rate of nights booked on an origin basis in our expansion markets continues to be twice that of our core markets over the last 12 months. Our strategy includes attracting new users to the platform, and we’re seeing big wins in some of our key markets. First-time bookers increased over 20% in Japan and nearly 50% in India on a year-over-year basis. 1 Flexible location carousels are currently available to a limited number of guests, with broader availability anticipated
